assertions.title: Allowed sellable sizes are visible before pack and enforced on write

steps.action: Inspect pre-pack read surfaces in `production_log_detail.json`, `unpacked_batches.json`, and `openapi_factory_contract.json`
steps.expected: The surviving reads expose `allowedSellableSizes` before packing.
steps.observed: `openapi_factory_contract.json` includes `allowedSellableSizes` on both `ProductionLogDetailDto` and unpacked-batch reads; the runtime snapshots show the field present on `GET /api/v1/factory/production/logs/1` and `GET /api/v1/factory/unpacked-batches`.

steps.action: Review the negative pack writes in `pack_missing_target.json` and `pack_header_contract.json`
steps.expected: Missing or unknown sellable targets fail closed instead of auto-creating or defaulting a target.
steps.observed: `pack_missing_target.json` returns `400` with `lines[0].childFinishedGoodId: Sellable size target is required`, and `pack_header_contract.json` returns `400 Sellable size target is not allowed for production batch PROD-20260325-001: 999` when the canonical header is present but the target is unknown.

assertions.title: Exactly one public pack write surface survives

steps.expected: Only `POST /api/v1/factory/packing-records` survives as a public pack mutation.
steps.observed: The path summary records `/api/v1/factory/packing-records=true` while `/api/v1/factory/pack=false` and `/api/v1/factory/packing-records/{productionLogId}/complete=false`.

steps.action: Cross-check runtime and contract-surface absence proofs
steps.expected: Retired pack routes are absent from runtime, endpoint inventories, and frontend handoff.
steps.observed: `removed_routes.json` shows both retired pack routes returning 404, and `pack_public_surfaces_precise.json` reports no precise live matches for either retired route while the surviving docs only mention them as retired behavior.

assertions.title: Pack idempotency is explicit and has no legacy header fallback

steps.action: Inspect the surviving OpenAPI header contract
steps.expected: The pack write advertises exactly one supported replay header: `Idempotency-Key`.
steps.observed: `openapi_factory_contract.json` and `OpenApiSnapshotIT` lock the `POST /api/v1/factory/packing-records` parameters to `Idempotency-Key` only.

steps.expected: Missing canonical header fails closed, and legacy headers do not act as supported fallbacks.
steps.observed: Requests without `Idempotency-Key` return `400 Idempotency-Key header is required`; `X-Idempotency-Key` returns `400 ... not supported ... use Idempotency-Key`; `X-Request-Id` returns the same fail-closed contract; a request with the canonical header advances to business validation and rejects only the unknown sellable target.

steps.action: Review targeted packaging-material test coverage and the batch-pack Maven log
steps.expected: Successful pack execution consumes packaging materials, and missing/insufficient packaging materials fail closed.
steps.observed: `packaging-material-test.log` shows `PackagingMaterialServiceTest` passing 18 tests, and the batch-pack Maven log records `FactoryPackagingCostingIT` plus `ProductionLogPackingStatusRegressionIT` with `Posting packing journal: reference=...-PACKMAT`, proving packaging-material accounting/movement happens on pack.

steps.action: Inspect the real-runtime seed limitation note
steps.expected: If the default validation seed cannot prove positive pack consumption at runtime, the validation should fall back to deterministic suite coverage rather than faking fixtures.
steps.observed: `packaging_mapping_seed_limit.json` shows the default seed rejects packaging mappings with `Raw material must be of type PACKAGING`, confirming why positive consumption proof uses the targeted Maven suites instead of the empty packaging seed.

steps.action: Review the targeted pack service/controller/e2e coverage bundle
steps.expected: Successful pack execution creates finished-goods inventory truth and linked postings.
steps.observed: `targeted_batch_pack_tests_summary.json` shows `PackingServiceTest`, `PackingControllerTest`, and `FactoryPackagingCostingIT` all passing in the same validation run.

steps.action: Inspect positive pack/service test names and e2e log signals
steps.expected: The surviving route registers finished-good output as part of the pack action.
steps.observed: `PackingServiceTest` includes `recordPacking_postsViaAccountingFacade` and verifies finished-good batch registration, while `FactoryPackagingCostingIT` logs the paired `...-PACKMAT` and final pack journals in the successful pack flow.

assertions.title: Pack updates batch packing status monotonically

steps.action: Inspect the runtime batch state before packing
steps.expected: The batch starts in `READY_TO_PACK` before any pack mutation.
steps.observed: `production_log_detail.json` and `unpacked_batches.json` both show `status=READY_TO_PACK` with zero packed quantity before pack completion.

steps.expected: The surviving route moves the batch from `READY_TO_PACK` to `PARTIAL_PACKED` and then to `FULLY_PACKED` without a second mutation.
steps.observed: The regression test asserts `PARTIAL_PACKED` after the first pack and `FULLY_PACKED` after the final pack; the targeted Maven run passed that suite and logged the corresponding successful pack journals.

assertions.title: The surviving pack action handles completion without a second public mutation

steps.expected: Operators do not need a second public completion route.
steps.observed: `openapi_factory_contract.json` marks `/api/v1/factory/packing-records/{productionLogId}/complete=false`, `removed_routes.json` returns 404 for that route, and `pack_public_surfaces_precise.json` shows docs describing it only as retired.

steps.action: Review the surviving pack-flow regression and service coverage
steps.expected: The single `POST /api/v1/factory/packing-records` action can reach `FULLY_PACKED` on its own.
steps.observed: `ProductionLogPackingStatusRegressionIT` reaches `FULLY_PACKED` through repeated surviving pack calls, and `PackingServiceTest.recordPacking_marksLogFullyPackedWhenFinalQuantityIsRecorded` verifies the final state without any second public mutation.

frictions.description: The shared `validation-seed` runtime does not start with PACKAGING-typed raw materials or seeded size variants, so a positive live `POST /api/v1/factory/packing-records` proof cannot be completed from the default seed alone.
frictions.resolution: Used deterministic targeted Maven proof (`PackagingMaterialServiceTest`, `PackingServiceTest`, `FactoryPackagingCostingIT`, `ProductionLogPackingStatusRegressionIT`) for positive pack completion/consumption/status assertions while keeping negative contract probes on the real seeded API.
